# SqlArtisan

> A type-safe SQL query builder for C# (.NET 8). You write SQL-like C# and it
> produces the SQL string plus its bind parameters. The SQL you write is the SQL
> that runs — cross-database portability is a deliberate non-goal. Supported
> dialects: MySQL, Oracle, PostgreSQL, SQLite, SQL Server (default: PostgreSQL).

Each reference page pairs the C# you write with the exact SQL it emits. When
generating code, pick the API for the target DBMS; do not expect a single call to
be rewritten across dialects.

## Docs

- [Overview & Quick Start](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/README.md): Why, installation, first query, configuration, performance.
- [Reference Home](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/README.md): Index of all statements, expressions, functions, and the analyzer.

## Reference

- [Query Statements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/query-statements.md): SELECT (clauses, JOIN, set operators, pagination, FOR UPDATE), INSERT (multi-row, SET-like, INSERT…SELECT, UPSERT, MERGE), UPDATE, DELETE (correlated and joined forms; correlated forms require an aliased target), WITH/CTE, RETURNING, OUTPUT (SQL Server).
- [Expressions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/expressions.md): NULL, arithmetic, string concatenation (`||` vs `CONCAT`), conditions, JSON operators (`->`, `->>`, `#>`, `#>>`), array operators (`ARRAY[...]`, `&&`, `@>`, `<@`), full-text search (MySQL `MATCH ... AGAINST`, Oracle `CONTAINS`/`SCORE`, PostgreSQL `@@`/`TO_TSVECTOR`/`TO_TSQUERY`, SQLite FTS5 `MATCH`, SQL Server `CONTAINS`/`FREETEXT`), scalar subqueries, ALL/ANY/SOME, CASE, CAST, window functions, conditional aggregation, string aggregation, sequences.
- [Functions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/functions.md): Built-in function catalog — numeric, character, date/time, conversion, comparison, JSON (`JSON_EXTRACT`, `JSON_VALUE`, `JSON_QUERY`), full-text search, aggregate, string aggregation, window/analytic — plus bind-parameter types.
- [Analyzer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/analyzer.md): Opt-in Roslyn analyzer that warns when a construct doesn't match the configured target dialect — enabling it, rule reference (SQLA0001/SQLA0002), override keys, mixed-dialect projects, CI gates.
- [Query Cookbook](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/cookbook.md): Realistic end-to-end queries with the exact emitted SQL, pinned by unit tests — reporting (top-N per group, window functions, recursive CTEs), dynamic search screens (optional filters, faceting), batch/UPSERT/MERGE DML.

## Guides

- [Comparison Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/comparison.md): How SqlArtisan compares to EF Core, linq2db, SqlKata, Dapper.SqlBuilder, and InterpolatedSql — philosophy, type safety, machine-verifiability, dialect handling, execution, performance, and when NOT to choose SqlArtisan.
- [Dapper + SqlArtisan from Scratch](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/guides/dapper-quickstart.md): The greenfield path — packages, table classes (generated or hand-written), executing queries through `SqlArtisan.Dapper`, pinning queries with tests.
- [Using SqlArtisan with AI Coding Assistants](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/guides/ai-assistants.md): Analyzer target configuration, exact-SQL test pinning, and an agent-rules snippet for generated queries.

## Optional

- [Versioning & Support](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/docs/versioning.md): SemVer commitment, breaking-change definition (including emitted-SQL changes), deprecation process, verified engine versions, support window.
- [CHANGELOG](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/main/CHANGELOG.md): Version history.
- [Design (ADRs)](https://github.com/h-tacayama/SqlArtisan/tree/main/docs/adr): Architecture decisions — SQL fidelity and dialect handling, public API shape, performance, the analyzer's design, and the validity-enforcement boundary for AI-assisted SQL.
